meta.title: Queen — Ear Clef
meta.description: Four writers, one impossible voice — the London band behind Bohemian Rhapsody, News of the World, and rock's greatest live twenty minutes.

hero.name: Queen
hero.identity: Opera in a rock band's hands — four songwriters and a frontman the stage still misses.

story.heading: The Story
paragraphs: Queen formed in London in 1970: Freddie Mercury on voice and piano, Brian May on a guitar he built with his father, Roger Taylor on drums, with John Deacon completing the lineup on bass. All four wrote hits — a rarity that gave the band its unusual range, from vaudeville to hard rock to funk.

A Night at the Opera (1975) brought international success and 'Bohemian Rhapsody', which held the UK number one for nine weeks and — with its promotional film — helped invent the music video as a form. News of the World (1977) supplied the stadiums with 'We Will Rock You' and 'We Are the Champions'; The Game (1980) added 'Another One Bites the Dust' and 'Crazy Little Thing Called Love'.

The live band may have been the greatest of its era, a case settled in twenty minutes at Live Aid in 1985 — routinely ranked among the finest performances in rock history. Freddie Mercury died on 24 November 1991, the day after publicly confirming he had AIDS; John Deacon retired from music in 1997.

May and Taylor have carried the songbook forward since 2004 under the Queen + banner — first with Paul Rodgers, and since 2011 with Adam Lambert — while the original records keep doing what they always did: making everyone in the room sing the parts they can't reach.

shows.upcomingNote: Queen + Adam Lambert have no shows currently scheduled — new dates are announced at queenonline.com.
